title: Wonder Novel Study Essay
description: Read chapters 1-8 of Wonder by R.J. Palacio. Write a 3-paragraph essay responding to the prompt: how does Auggie's first day at school shape his view of himself? Cite at least two moments from the text.

title: Grammar Practice: Commas and Compound Sentences
description: Complete the grammar packet on comma rules and compound sentences. Show your work on each item. Please staple pages before turning in.

title: Poetry Portfolio: Five Original Poems
description: Compile a portfolio of five original poems: one haiku, one free verse, one narrative, one simile-driven, one figurative-language showcase. Include a one-paragraph reflection on your writing choices.

title: Fractions and Decimals Unit Assessment
description: Unit assessment covering adding, subtracting, multiplying, and dividing fractions and converting between fractions and decimals. Calculators not permitted. Show all work in the space provided.

title: Ratios and Proportions Problem Set
description: Complete problems 1-20 on ratios, unit rates, and proportional relationships. Include a labeled diagram for any word problems. Due Friday at end of class.

title: Geometry Quiz: Area, Perimeter, and Volume
description: Short quiz covering area of triangles and quadrilaterals, perimeter of composite figures, and volume of rectangular prisms. Show work on all problems.

title: Cell Diagram Lab: Plant and Animal Cells
description: Label a plant cell and an animal cell using microscope observations. Identify differences and explain the function of each organelle in a short paragraph.

title: Volcano and Plate Tectonics Project
description: Build a 3D volcano model and prepare a two-page write-up explaining the type of volcano modeled, the plate boundary that produced it, and a real-world case study. Presentations run March 3-6 in class.

title: Ancient Mesopotamia Timeline
description: Create an illustrated timeline of ten major events in Mesopotamian history from 4000 BCE to 500 BCE. Each event should include a date, one-sentence summary, and a source citation.

title: Ancient Egypt Research Paper
description: Write a two-page research paper on one topic from Ancient Egypt: pyramids, mummification, the Nile River economy, or hieroglyphics. Include three cited sources in MLA format.

title: Second Quarter Fitness Log
description: Track daily activity minutes and one heart-rate check per week for the second quarter. Submit the completed log signed by a parent or guardian on December 19.

title: Charcoal Self-Portrait
description: Complete a charcoal self-portrait using mirror observation and the value scale technique from class. Include a written reflection on your process and one thing you would change next time.

title: Winter Term Goal-Setting Reflection
description: Fill out the goal-setting worksheet for winter term. Choose one academic goal, one habit goal, and one community goal. Bring the completed worksheet to advisory next Friday for share-outs.

title: Charlotte's Web Chapter Book Report
description: Finish Charlotte's Web by E.B. White and complete the book report packet: main characters, setting, a favorite quote, and a drawing of your favorite scene. Neat handwriting matters!

title: Personal Narrative Essay
description: Write a one-page personal narrative about a memorable day. Use a strong beginning, middle, and end. Include at least three sensory details and one line of dialogue.

title: Multiplication Tables Mastery Quiz (0-10)
description: Timed multiplication quiz covering tables 0 through 10. Students who pass all 100 facts under three minutes will move on to the extended (11-12) tables.

title: Fractions on a Number Line Worksheet Set
description: Complete both worksheets on placing fractions on a number line and comparing fractions with like denominators. Color the fraction models neatly and show your reasoning.

title: Habitat Diorama Project
description: Choose a Utah habitat (Great Basin, alpine, wetland, or riparian) and build a shoebox diorama showing at least three plants and three animals. Include a labeled key on the back.

title: Two-Week Weather Journal
description: Record daily temperature, cloud cover, and precipitation for two weeks. Draw a graph of your temperature data at the end. Journals will be shared out in small groups.

title: Utah Landmarks Poster
description: Choose one Utah landmark (natural or human-made) and create a poster with a labeled photo or drawing, three facts, and one reason it matters to the state. Present in class January 22.

title: Fall Movement Journal
description: Log at least twenty minutes of movement per day (walking, biking, playing, dancing) for four weeks. Turn in the family-signed journal at the end of the unit.

title: Recorder Practice Video: Hot Cross Buns
description: Record a short video of yourself playing Hot Cross Buns on the recorder, using the finger positions from class. Upload the video privately to your Drive folder.

title: Watercolor Landscape Painting
description: Paint a watercolor landscape inspired by a place you love in Utah. Use the layering and wet-on-wet techniques we practiced in class. Attach a title card with your name and a one-sentence description.

title: Novel Response Essay: Bridge to Terabithia
description: Archived assignment from Fifth Grade Language Arts 2024-25. Two-page response essay on Bridge to Terabithia by Katherine Paterson. Includes character analysis and theme discussion.

title: End-of-Year Math Skills Assessment
description: Archived assignment from Fifth Grade Math 2024-25. Cumulative assessment covering multi-digit multiplication and division, decimals, and volume of rectangular prisms.

title: Plant Growth Lab Report
description: Archived assignment from Fifth Grade Science 2024-25. Six-week bean plant growth experiment comparing sunlight and shade conditions. Includes hypothesis, data table, graph, and conclusion.

title: Final Book Report: Magic Tree House
description: Archived assignment from Second Grade Reading 2024-25. Illustrated book report on any Magic Tree House title. Includes favorite character, setting, and a drawing.

title: Skip Counting Mastery
description: Archived assignment from Second Grade Math 2024-25. Timed practice on skip counting by 2s, 5s, and 10s to 200. Second graders showed strong mastery in the final round.

title: Simple Machines Project
description: Archived assignment from Second Grade Science 2024-25. Small-group project designing a Rube Goldberg-style machine using at least three simple machines. Includes a labeled diagram.
